commit.message:
fix: review 3 — USDC-only policy, redirect block, streaming reader, break circular dep

R1-1 (High): PaymentPolicy now checks r.asset === USDC mainnet mint.
  Non-USDC SPL tokens are rejected regardless of amount.
R1-2 (High): redirect: "error" in RequestInit blocks 302/307
  redirect-based allowlist bypass.
R2-① (High): Replace response.text() with streaming readBodySafe()
  that enforces actual byte limit (4MB) regardless of Content-Length.
R2-③ (Medium): Extract fetchMap to state.ts, breaking the
  index.ts ↔ fetch-x402.ts circular dependency.

35 tests pass (was 32). +3 USDC asset validation tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve hired agent skills and preload skill badge count (#299)

* feat: add email-based authentication system with login page and verification flow

Implement complete email/password auth with verification codes, Turnstile captcha,
rate limiting, and a new login page. Update landing page with auth integration,
rewrite docs philosophy page, and add i18n support for auth flows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(auth): clean up auth endpoints and fix reset-password bug

- Remove broken _find_user_email (Casdoor ignores field/value filter,
  always returned first user in org regardless of query email)
- Fix send-reset-code: use Casdoor's native method=forget instead of
  manual user lookup that sent codes to wrong email
- Fix reset-password: use request email directly, not looked-up email
- Simplify send-code: detect existing users via Casdoor's signup error
  response instead of unreliable get-users API
- Simplify login/password: reduce username candidates from 4 to 2
- Extract shared helpers: _require_casdoor, _resolve_origin,
  _to_user_info, _validate_and_login, _casdoor_code_login
- Move 'import requests' to module level (was duplicated 7 times)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): use Casdoor get-user API for user existence check and make app owner configurable

Replace unreliable signup-error-based detection with direct Casdoor
GET /api/get-user?owner=org&email=email lookup. Add ApplicationOwner
config field to avoid hardcoded "admin" in applicationId.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: preserve hired agent skills and preload skill badge count (#299)

* fix: preload skill badge count before panel open

* fix: preserve published skills when hiring marketplace agents

* fix: address sourcery feedback for skill fork and error fallback

* fix: resolve memory panel hover scroll trap

* fix: break infinite logout loop caused by 401 on push-unsubscribe

logout() fires an unauthenticated push-unsubscribe DELETE which returns
401, triggering the HTTP client's auto-logout handler, which calls
logout() again — ad infinitum. Guard the 401 handler to only trigger
logout when a token still exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: i18n login page tagline with civilization metaphor across en/zh/ja

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add interactive PDF source viewer in soldier modals

Each soldier's detail modal now shows an embedded PDF viewer pointing
to the exact line in the original source document. Features include
zoom, page navigation, auto-scroll to position, and a yellow highlight
bar. Uses react-pdf v9 with lazy loading so the PDF.js bundle (~500KB)
only loads when a modal opens.

- Extract PDF page/position metadata for all 28,855 soldiers
- Copy 7 source PDFs (~37.5 MB) to public/pdfs/
- Create shared SoldierModal component (replaces duplicate code)
- Add PdfViewer component with toolbar and highlight
- Update soldier JSON files with pdf_page, pdf_y, pdf_file fields

fix: add Log Analytics fallback for unreliable az container logs (#67)

az container logs returns HTTP 500 intermittently for VNet-integrated
containers (known Azure ACI limitation). Log Analytics already receives
all logs via ContainerInsights diagnostic config.

Changes:
- verify.sh: agent_logs() and proxy_logs() now try az container logs
  first, fall back to Log Analytics query when it fails
- Fixed Test 8 table name: ContainerLog → ContainerInstanceLog_CL
- Added la_logs() and la_workspace() helpers

Closes #67

commit.message:
Fix weather export: set windowWidth/height to activate lg: breakpoints

html2canvas renders using the original viewport dimensions, so Tailwind
lg: classes never activated on mobile. Setting windowWidth:1600 and
windowHeight:1000 makes the cloned DOM render as if on a wide screen,
plus width/height force the canvas to 1600x1000.

commit.message:
fix(renderer): uniform opacity and background color for TUI programs

Add dedicated replace-blend GPU pipeline for background rect quads to
prevent alpha accumulation with semi-transparent windows. Track bg
vertices separately in the compositor, force full damage on opacity
changes, preserve opacity across OSC 11 background color changes, and
treat cells matching the theme background as default so TUI programs
track dynamic background updates.

Add CIRCULAR_DEP_DESIGN.md: C constant dependency analysis

Documents every cross-file constant dependency in NetHack headers,
identifying the pure leaf headers and their JS equivalents.

Key finding: the C constant dep graph is shallow (12 files, 1-2 levels
deep, no cycles). JS module cycles are function-only and not a constant
initialization problem. The leaf tier (config.js, monsters.js,
objects.js, symbols.js, hacklib.js) must never import from gameplay
modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Best Bets summary section with star ratings

New BestBets component appears below Model Lines in all 3 sports (NBA,
NHL, NCAAM). Shows up to 3 bets ranked by model confidence:

Bet types computed:
- ML: consensus probability (home or away, whichever model favors)
- Spread: P(home covers) via normal CDF with sport-specific sigma
  NBA: sigma=16.3 (sqrt2 * 11.5) | NCAAM: 14.1 | NHL: 2.4 (Poisson)
  Formula: normalCDF((modelMargin + homeSpread) / sigma)
- Over/Under: P(over) via normalCDF((modelTotal - postedLine) / sigma)

Star ratings:
  5 stars = 81%+   4 stars = 61-80%   3 stars = 41-60%

BestBets only renders spread/total rows when those fields are filled in.
With just ML available it shows 1 row; with all three filled, shows 3
rows sorted by confidence descending.

Also adds normalCDF helper (Abramowitz & Stegun approx, error < 7.5e-8).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

canary: switch MFU gate to p50 over trailing window (#3279) Ref: #3278 ## Summary - Accumulate MFU samples over a trailing window (last 500 steps) and write distribution stats (p10, p50, p90, mean, stddev, count) to the tracker summary. - Switch the canary gate from last-step `throughput/mfu >= 25.0` to `throughput/p50_mfu >= 22.0`. ## Motivation The canary MFU gate checks a single terminal MFU sample. At the canary's baseline throughput (~2.5 it/s on v5p-8), the expected MFU is ~24.9% — right at the 25% threshold. The gate is a coin flip on whether the last step happens to be fast or slow. Using the median over a trailing window eliminates this sensitivity. The threshold is lowered to 22% to provide comfortable margin above noise while still catching real regressions. ## Design notes - **Window size 500**: large enough for stable percentiles, bounded in memory. The canary runs 3814 steps, so the final window covers the last ~13% of training — well past compilation warmup. - **`log_summary` every step**: necessary because the callback doesn't know which step is the last. The trainer calls `run_hooks(force=True)` at end of training for the final flush. - **Threshold 22%**: historical baseline shows sustained MFU of ~24.9% at 2.5 it/s. 22% gives ~12% margin for normal variance. The p50 naturally filters outlier steps. ## Test plan - [x] `uv run pytest tests/test_validate_canary_metrics.py` — passes - [ ] Canary triggered on branch: https://github.com/marin-community/marin/actions/runs/22732424437 - [ ] Verify `throughput/p50_mfu` appears in `tracker_metrics.jsonl` + W&B after run
Key naming: why throughput/p50_mfu not throughput/mfu/p50 W&B can't have `throughput/mfu` be both a scalar (per-step log) and a namespace (`throughput/mfu/p50`, ...) simultaneously. Using hierarchy would require renaming the existing per-step key, breaking `setup_wandb_workspace.py`, `parse_wandb_runs.py`, existing dashboards, and historical run data. Not worth the churn for this PR.
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
fix(build): use git tag --points-at for deterministic version when multiple tags share a commit

Replaces `git describe --tags --exact-match HEAD` with
`git tag --points-at HEAD --sort=-version:refname` so that when multiple
tags point at the same commit the highest semver tag is always chosen
rather than an arbitrary one.
